Package de.uka.ilkd.key.rule

Class RewriteTaclet

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.Taclet
de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.FindTaclet
de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.RewriteTaclet
All Implemented Interfaces:
Rule, HasOrigin, Named, EqualsModProofIrrelevancy
Direct Known Subclasses:
InfFlowContractAppTaclet
public class RewriteTaclet extends FindTaclet
A RewriteTaclet represents a taclet, whose find can be matched against any term in the sequent no matter where it occurs. The only constraint to be fulfilled is that the term matches the structure described by the term of the find-part.

  • Field Details

    • NONE

      public static final int NONE
      does not pose state restrictions on valid matchings
      See Also:

    • SAME_UPDATE_LEVEL

      public static final int SAME_UPDATE_LEVEL
      all taclet constituents must appear in the same state (and not below a modality (for efficiency reasons))

    • IN_SEQUENT_STATE

      public static final int IN_SEQUENT_STATE
      all taclet constituents must be in the same state as the sequent
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • ANTECEDENT_POLARITY

      public static final int ANTECEDENT_POLARITY
      If the surrounding formula has been decomposed completely, the find-term will NOT appear on the SUCcedent. The formula wellformed(h) in wellformed(h) ==> or in ==> wellformed(h) -> (inv(h) = inv(h2)) or in ==> \if(b) \then(!wellformed(h)) \else(!wellformed(h2)) has antecedent polarity. The formula wellformed(h) in wellformed(h) <-> wellformed(h2) ==> has NO antecedent polarity.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • SUCCEDENT_POLARITY

      public static final int SUCCEDENT_POLARITY
      If the surrounding formula has been decomposed completely, the find-term will NOT appear on the ANTEcedent. The formula wellformed(h) in ==> wellformed(h) or in wellformed(h) -> (inv(h) = inv(h2)) ==> or in \if(b) \then(!wellformed(h)) \else(!wellformed(h2)) ==> has succedent polarity. The formula wellformed(h) in wellformed(h) <-> wellformed(h2) ==> has NO succedent polarity.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • RewriteTaclet

      public RewriteTaclet(Name name, TacletApplPart applPart, ImmutableList<TacletGoalTemplate> goalTemplates, ImmutableList<RuleSet> ruleSets, TacletAttributes attrs, Term find, ImmutableMap<SchemaVariable,TacletPrefix> prefixMap, int p_applicationRestriction, ChoiceExpr choices, ImmutableSet<TacletAnnotation> tacletAnnotations)
      creates a Schematic Theory Specific Rule (Taclet) with the given parameters that represents a rewrite rule.
      Parameters:
      name - the Name of the Taclet
      applPart - the TacletApplPart that contains the application part of an Taclet that is the if-sequent, the variable conditions
      goalTemplates - a list of goal descriptions.
      ruleSets - a list of rule sets for the Taclet
      attrs - the TacletAttributes; these are boolean values indicating a noninteractive or recursive use of the Taclet.
      find - the find term of the Taclet
      prefixMap - an ImmutableMap that contains the prefix for each SchemaVariable in the Taclet
      p_applicationRestriction - an int defining state restrictions of the taclet (required for location check)
      choices - the SetOf to which this taclet belongs to

    • RewriteTaclet

      public RewriteTaclet(Name name, TacletApplPart applPart, ImmutableList<TacletGoalTemplate> goalTemplates, ImmutableList<RuleSet> ruleSets, TacletAttributes attrs, Term find, ImmutableMap<SchemaVariable,TacletPrefix> prefixMap, int p_applicationRestriction, ChoiceExpr choices, boolean surviveSymbExec, ImmutableSet<TacletAnnotation> tacletAnnotations)

  • Method Details

    • createAndInitializeExecutor

      protected void createAndInitializeExecutor()
      Specified by:
      createAndInitializeExecutor in class Taclet

    • ignoreTopLevelUpdates

      public boolean ignoreTopLevelUpdates()
      this method is used to determine if top level updates are allowed to be ignored. This is the case if we have an Antec or SuccTaclet but not for a RewriteTaclet
      Specified by:
      ignoreTopLevelUpdates in class FindTaclet
      Returns:
      true if top level updates shall be ignored

    • getApplicationRestriction

      public int getApplicationRestriction()
      returns the int encoding the kind of state restriction this rewrite taclet must obey
      Returns:
      the int encoding the kind of state restriction this rewrite taclet must obey

    • checkPrefix

      public MatchConditions checkPrefix(PosInOccurrence p_pos, MatchConditions p_mc)
      For taclets with getSameUpdatePrefix (), collect the updates above p_pos and add them to the update context of the instantiations object p_mc.
      Returns:
      the new instantiations with the additional updates, or null, if program modalities appear above p_pos
